For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check where the player piece moved after 3 RIGHT actions. I need to track the movement pattern and understand the goal.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position after 3 RIGHT moves
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
player = [n for n in nodes if n['id'] == 32][0]
print(f"Player boundary now: {player['boundary']}")

# Check all transitions to see movement pattern
for i, t in enumerate(transitions):
    before_n = [n for n in t.before_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['id'] == 32][0]
    after_n = [n for n in t.after_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['id'] == 32][0]
    bc = (sum(p[0] for p in before_n['boundary'])/len(before_n['boundary']), 
          sum(p[1] for p in before_n['boundary'])/len(before_n['boundary']))
    ac = (sum(p[0] for p in after_n['boundary'])/len(after_n['boundary']), 
          sum(p[1] for p in after_n['boundary'])/len(after_n['boundary']))
    print(f"Transition {i} ({t.action}): player center {bc} -> {ac}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Interesting - the player only moved once (from 10.5 to 11.4) on the first RIGHT, then stopped moving. This suggests there might be a blocking obstacle, or the movement is different than I thought. Let me check the ASCII around the player's position to see what's blocking it, and also try other directions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what's around the player and try different directions
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
# Player is around rows 38-43, cols 8-13
print("Area around player (rows 36-45, cols 6-16):")
for r in range(36, 45):
    print(f"  {r:2d}: {lines[r][6:17]}")

# Try UP to see if player moves that way
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

  37: ccbcbcccccc

  39: ccggggggccc
  40: ccggggNNccc
  41: ccggggNNccc
  42: ccggggggccc
  43: ccggggggccc
  44: ccccccccccc
